eligibility: Guidelines specify up to $10,000/year per project, paid 50% on signing/50% on final report; 2025 average award was ~$5,900/project. No institution, degree-level, dissertation, or postdoctoral restriction found.
notes: Eligibility unclear — verify on website. Amount/deadline figures conflict across sources - recommend confirming exact current-cycle deadline/cap directly on afpglobal.org. Added 2026-06-30 (batch 2); source: https://afpglobal.org/research-reports/afp-foundation-philanthropy-research-grants-initiatives. Imported from personal research tracker; verify all details before applying.

organization: W.K. Kellogg Foundation (with Center for Creative Leadership)
name: WKKF Community Leadership Network Fellowship
amount: Funded — amount not specified in source; verify on website
other_benefits: Leadership-development fellowship for community leaders improving outcomes for children/youth/families; cross-sector program spanning business, government, faith, academia, nonprofit, and philanthropic sectors. Time commitment: 18 months, part-time - six in-person multi-day gatherings + three virtual sessions + ~6-12 hrs/month; designed alongside a fellow's current job.
eligibility: $20,000 taxable stipend; requires 5+ years relevant professional experience; must reside in contiguous US or bordering Tribal nations. No degree/enrollment-status language found, no explicit student exclusion.
notes: Eligibility unclear — verify on website. Geographic eligibility is broad (multi-state + Tribal nations), not a single-institution restriction. Added 2026-06-30 (batch 2); source: https://wkkfcln.org/. Imported from personal research tracker; verify all details before applying.

name: Career Pathways
deadline: Most recent deadline 2026-03-02 (passed); next cycle expected ~March 2027
amount: Unfunded / non-monetary
other_benefits: Leadership-development program for mid-to-senior-level philanthropy/grantmaking professionals preparing for executive roles, combining live sessions, executive coaching, and peer circles over 10 months. Time commitment: 10 months, part-time/non-residential - four learning sessions, ~40-50 hrs of programming, alongside current job with employer support.
eligibility: Must be a current employee of a grantmaking organization/foundation/philanthropic institution with 5-7 years mid-to-senior-level experience, plus a recommendation from home institution. Pay-to-participate: $5,600-$7,550 members, $9,300 nonmembers; need-based scholarship can cover tuition.
notes: Listed Funded=No because participants/employers pay a program fee rather than receiving a stipend, though scholarship aid is available. Added 2026-06-30 (batch 2); source: https://cof.org/career-pathways. Imported from personal research tracker; verify all details before applying.
